{{Quote|Santor, there is really nothing to decide. You are the best among us. You should go.|[[Margan]]|<ref name="SW87" />}}
'''Santor''' was a diplomat from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shawken]], who helped lead Shawken's temporary government after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] abandoned the world following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Santor hosted a delegation from the [[Alliance of Free Planets]] led by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. At Skywalker's entreaty, Santor represented his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] at the [[First Conference of Free Peoples]] on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Oh, I am most terribly sorry about this... I never anticipated that any of the old ruins were still active... According to our history, our ancestors left behind some dreadful weapons...|Santor, to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SW87" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Santor was a native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shawken]], home to one of the oldest-known civilizations in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. A prominent [[Politics/Legends|politician]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Santor became part of Shawken's ruling council after [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces fled the world in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], not long after the Empire's climactic [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. He then served as the leader of a delegation that met with a group of diplomats from the newly formed [[Alliance of Free Planets]], who traveled to Shawken in order to invite local leaders to the upcoming [[First Conference of Free Peoples]]. Santor welcomed the diplomats, led by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], into his home, where Santor was entreated to personally represent Shawken.<ref name="SW87" />

However, a friendly meeting was soon interrupted by an urgent message: several of Skywalker's traveling companions, [[Rik Duel/Legends|Rik Duel]], [[Dani/Legends|Dani]] and [[Chihdo/Legends|Chihdo]], had become trapped while exploring Shawken's ancient catacombs and inadvertently activated the [[Shawken Device]], a long-forgotten [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] purported to have the power to destroy the universe. Santor led Skywalker and his retinue to the ruins, where the [[Iskalonian]] [[Kiro]] was able to shut off the superweapon before its effects, if any, were loosed upon the galaxy.<ref name="SW87" /> After the crisis was resolved, Santor personally represented Shawken at the First Conference of Free Peoples on Endor, listening to speeches from Alliance leaders [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], among others.<ref name="SW90">[[Star Wars (1977) 90|''Star Wars'' (1977) 90]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|We want none but the best speaking for us... besides, the entire council could benefit from your wisdom...|[[Unidentified Shawkenese woman|One of Santor's aides]]|<ref name="SW87" />}}
A skilled diplomat, Santor was considered by his fellows to be the wisest and best among the [[Shawkenese]] government, and was a gracious and personable host to visiting dignitaries. Although personally opposed to Imperial rule, Santor took the long view of political power—Shawken's civilization was so ancient, he reasoned, that it would long outlive the Empire and likely whatever came after. Santor was passionate about his homeworld, and very knowledgeable about its long and storied history.<ref name="SW87" />

==Behind the scenes==
Santor first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 87|''Star Wars'' 87]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]], illustrated by [[Tom Palmer]] and released in [[1984]].<ref name="SW87" />
